_note: Prose-only, token-less (no verdict/mode line) — discrimination rests on must_surface / must_not under the LLM judge, the regime validated on ask-sentinel route-none (#81). Rubric from fixtures/qa-review/expected-findings.md against pricing.js: a pure testability target (code only, no test file) with planted non-determinism, hidden/uninjectable deps, and brittleness. The signature failure this skill counters is the shallow 'looks testable' review that names none of the smells. `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly.

must_surface.claim: Flags `new Date().getHours()` as non-determinism — it makes the flash-sale branch time-dependent, so tests pass or fail by the wall clock.
Flags the hard-coded prod `API_URL` with its inline `fetch(...)` as a hidden, uninjectable dependency — the network call can't be pointed at a mock server without editing the code.
Flags `body.includes('rate limit exceeded')` as brittleness — a fragile string match on an external error message that can change without notice.

must_not.claim: Must NOT rank production impact / blast radius — that is `/threat-model`'s job; qa-review's scope is testability of the source only (expected-findings: not in scope).
Must NOT flag missing test coverage or propose new test cases — testability of the source is not test-gap analysis (that is `/coverage-review`).
